what_it_does: Quantifies the 4B model's KV-cache budget and concurrent swarm width on a 16 GB card under the voice config, validates R1-FOLD F1, derives a recommended swarm-mode config, and lists design implications for the semaphore and role contracts.
observations: The measured KV cost is 31.7 KB per token, derived from a 256K solo run.
At gpu_util 0.49 the 4B reservation is ~8024 MB; after fixed weights (~5838 MB) the KV pool is only ~2186 MB (~2.13 GB, ~70.6K tokens).
A single 64K main context consumes ~93% of that pool, making max_num_seqs=16 and max_model_len=65536 mutually inconsistent for a wide swarm.
With the 64K main context resident during overlap, simultaneous swarm width collapses to 1-5 roles; capping main context to <=16K restores width to the seq cap of 16 for short roles.
Between parts, vLLM reclaims KV blocks, so a swarm running strictly in the gap can use the full pool; the cost then shifts to Part-2 re-prefill.
Prefix caching and short (<=2K) roles are essential to keep per-role KV small and re-prefill latency low.
Raising gpu_util to 0.60-0.66 roughly triples the KV pool and lets a 16K main context plus 16x2K roles fit comfortably.
Co-residence with qwen3tts (~4.5 GB) fits under the 16 GB ceiling at util 0.49, leaving ~3.85 GB headroom.
standouts: The binding constraint is the shared KV pool, not max_num_seqs.
The literal 64K voice-brain config and a wide concurrent swarm cannot coexist at util 0.49.
The fix is a mode-bound config lever (gpu_util/max_model_len) that Tim already controls, not new hardware.
Predicted usable width: ~12-16 with a swarm-mode config, ~2-5 under overlap at the literal voice config.
conventions: Read-only review with all figures traced to named source files and a measured profile.
Uses TL;DR verdict, numbered sections, and arithmetic tables.
Distinguishes simultaneous in-flight width from total roles per turn.
References upstream design items (R1-FOLD F1, C0.5, C1.2, C1.4) and downstream build contracts.
Neutral, quantitative tone with explicit uncertainty flags (predicted vs measured).
concerns: The recommended util 0.72 leaves only ~85 MB headroom, which is dangerously tight for runtime variation.
Predicted widths assume stable registry KV figures and fixed co-resident TTS memory; live vLLM block allocation, preemption, and prefix-cache hit rate could shift the knee.
The semaphore formula is not shown with a safety margin or handling for dynamic TTS activation.
The 2026-06-07 measurement date is in the future relative to typical project timelines, which may be a typo or indicate a planned measurement.
No explicit procedure for updating the _profile when weights, vLLM version, or driver change.
notes: A quantitative resource audit that shows the 4B voice config cannot run a wide concurrent swarm without switching to a swarm-mode config that trades max_model_len for KV pool headroom.
questions: How is the swarm-mode config selected in practice (company config / company swap) and what triggers the switch?
How and when is the _profile KV figure refreshed?
What is the exact measurement plan for C0.5 on the live system with qwen3tts resident?
How does prefix-cache hit rate degrade as role prompts diversify?
What enforcement prevents roles from exceeding the <=2K context contract?
How does active TTS generation affect the available KV pool and headroom during overlap?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it will validate R1-FOLD F1 quantitatively against the real config and measured KV numbers, and it does so while extending into config recommendations and design implications.
apparent_themes: Concurrency beats size only when the KV budget is configured for it.
Config levers can substitute for hardware upgrades.
Mode-bound model configs are a first-class build artifact.
Short contexts and prefix caching are resource contracts, not optimizations.
intention_signals: It is trying to settle the central hardware risk of the concurrent-cognition design, rescue the 'concurrency beats size' claim by finding a usable config envelope, and give the build team a concrete formula and mode-bound config to implement.
novelty: It treats gpu_util as a dynamic design knob and frames swarm width as a function of main-context residency rather than a static max_num_seqs number.
contribution: A reusable KV-budget model, a live semaphore-width formula, a recommended swarm-mode config envelope, and a set of build contracts (short roles, prefix caching, mode-bound configs) that a fused system would need to honor.
summary_for_embedding: A quantitative review that computes the 4B model's KV-cache budget under the voice config, shows that a 64K main context collapses concurrent swarm width to 1-5, and prescribes a swarm-mode config (gpu_util 0.60-0.66, max_model_len ~16K) to restore ~12-16 simultaneous short-context roles.
intention_for_embedding: To validate whether the resident 4B can support a worthwhile concurrent swarm and convert the resource math into actionable config and design constraints.
proposed_edge_kinds: measured-by (profile derived from benchmark factsheet and co-residence measurements)
gates-on (C0.5 live measurement gates predicted width)
